text: The surgeon begins adhesiolysis using traction and counter traction techniques. Cold scissors are employed to dissect along the white line indicating the plane between structures, ensuring peritoneal sparing and minimizing blood loss while avoiding injury to adjacent tissues.

The surgeon visualizes the uterus and injects vasopressin into the myelodendron, using a dilution of twenty units in one hundred ml or forty units in two hundred ml, to aid in adhesiolysis and reduce blood loss.
